title: Living in Boulder vlog 12 - 19 -  2017: Google Earthing apartments of region 5 of Ancient Rome

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs at 5:40am while high, describing his ongoing project of mapping ancient Rome's Region 5 in Google Earth. He explains his process of using the Regionaries (an ancient Roman building census) to accurately place apartments, houses, baths, and other structures, and reflects on the project as his life mission to visually illustrate historical reality.
enrichment.speaker: kyle
topics: apartments and housing in ancient Rome

stances.position: Wants exact building counts per the Regionaries, visualized with color-coding and scale — good enough detail to imagine the city without being impossibly precise
Predicts fully detailed virtual Rome is 30-40 years away; sees his own work as a valid intermediate step with permanent value
Believes illustrating historical numbers and space visually is his calling — something he gravitated toward naturally

claims.subject: melatonin
claims.claim: I guess I'll take a melatonin because I'm not tired at all.

claims.subject: marijuana
claims.claim: The batch I have right now isn't really that strong; it doesn't pack tight like the stuff I had before.

claims.subject: marijuana
claims.claim: The previous batch was like powder condensed—you could squeeze it with your finger and it would pop, leaving powder behind.

claims.subject: Regional Academy of Cattleogues
claims.claim: There's this thing called the Regional Academy of Cattleogues, which I pretended didn't exist in my head until recently.

claims.subject: Regional Academy of Cattleogues
claims.claim: The Regional Academy of Cattleogues was active from the study of 68 to 354, and I had the last year of it through this fort.

claims.subject: buildings
claims.claim: The Regional Academy of Cattleogues lists how many buildings of each kind exist in a beach region, like embolls, apartments, houses, green depots, baths, and systems.

claims.subject: Google Earth
claims.claim: I needed to find a way to build a city on Google Earth that illustrates everything in detail, like a cartoon but not too tiny.

claims.subject: houses
claims.claim: I designed houses based on an average nice big house plan, with all rooms divided and a traditional layout.

claims.subject: houses
claims.claim: I have five meters of the house design, which I think is enough, but I can modify it for the next region.

claims.subject: houses
claims.claim: I have different sizes of houses, including little houses and standard cookie-cutter houses.

claims.subject: apartments
claims.claim: I was figuring out how to illustrate the exact number of apartments, which was 2200 out of 3750 needed.

claims.subject: apartments
claims.claim: I decided to make 100 square foot apartments next to each other, as described on Wikipedia, where families could live.

claims.subject: virtual reality
claims.claim: In 30 or 40 years, we might have virtual reality where you can recreate a room exactly, like room number 6075 in a house.

claims.subject: visualization
claims.claim: I'm trying to create a way to visualize numbers and space in actual history so you can understand everything just by looking at it.

claims.subject: apartments
claims.claim: I have to do about 3750 individual apartments, which is a lot of work but great for visualizing the whole city.

claims.subject: timeline
claims.claim: I thought I'd be done by the end of April, but now I realize it might take longer because of all the apartments I have to do.
